The course is a form of tertiary education (Target 4.3) that develops research skills (Target 4.4) and addresses sustainability issues (Target 4.7).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: As an "International Business" seminar, it relates to economic productivity (Target 8.2). The competency on "socially responsible... issues" can cover decent work (Target 8.8).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's focus on "latest research direction & results" and enhancing "research and publication capability" directly supports scientific research and innovation (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The competency to identify "ethical" and "socially responsible" issues in business could tangentially relate to reducing inequalities of outcome (Target 10.3).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core competency B explicitly requires students to "understand and identify... sustainability issues," which aligns with encouraging sustainable business practices (Target 12.6).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The focus on "ethical... issues" in business can relate to reducing corruption (Target 16.5), and "Respect Copyright" supports the rule of law (Target 16.3).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course is an "academic exchange platform" inviting "experts and scholars," fostering knowledge-sharing partnerships (Target 17.16) in the field of international business.
